Module com.example._0tazo
Package com.example._0tazo.model

Class Deck

java.lang.Object
com.example._0tazo.model.Deck
public class Deck extends Object
Represents a double standard deck of 104 playing cards, composed of two complete 52-card sets â€” one with red backs and one with blue backs.

The deck is split into two piles:

  • Main deck: the draw pile players take cards from, initially face down.
  • Discard pile: where players send used cards, always face up.

When the main deck runs out of cards, the discard pile is automatically recycled: all cards except the last played are flipped face down, transferred to the main deck, and shuffled before play resumes. The last played card remains on top of the discard pile, as required by the game rules.

Usage example:


 Deck deck = new Deck();
 Card card = deck.dealCard();        // take a card face up (human player)
 Card hidden = deck.dealCard(false); // take a card face down (machine player)
 deck.discard(card);                 // send it to the discard pile
 Card top = deck.peekDiscard();      // see the top of the discard pile
See Also:

  • Field Details

    • SUITS

      private static final String[] SUITS
      The four standard suits of a playing card deck.

    • NUMBERS

      private static final String[] NUMBERS
      The thirteen standard card values using the long-form naming convention that matches the card image filenames (e.g. ace_of_spades.png).

    • mainDeck

      private final Stack<Card> mainDeck
      The main draw pile. Players take cards from this stack. Cards are stored face down until dealt.

    • discardPile

      private final Stack<Card> discardPile
      The discard pile. Cards played by players are pushed here face up. When the main deck is exhausted, all cards except the top one are recycled back into the main deck.

  • Constructor Details

    • Deck

      public Deck()
      Constructs a fully built and shuffled 104-card double-deck.

      Internally calls build() to populate the main deck with all 104 cards, then shuffle(Stack) to randomize their order. The discard pile starts empty.

  • Method Details

    • build

      private void build()
      Populates the main deck with 104 cards by iterating over all combinations of suits and numbers, pushing two copies of each card: one with a blue back and one with a red back.

      Card count breakdown:

         4 suits Ã— 13 numbers Ã— 2 back colors = 104 cards

    • shuffle

      private void shuffle(Stack<Card> stack)
      Shuffles the given stack in place.

      Since Collections.shuffle(java.util.List<?>) does not operate directly on Stack, the stack is temporarily converted to an ArrayList, shuffled, cleared, and repopulated.

      Parameters:
      stack - the Stack of Card objects to shuffle; must not be null

    • recycleDiscard

      private void recycleDiscard()
      Recycles the discard pile into the main deck.

      The top card of the discard pile (the last card played) is preserved and returned to the discard pile after recycling, as required by the game rules. All other cards are flipped face down, transferred to the main deck, and shuffled.

      This method does nothing if the discard pile has one card or fewer, since there would be nothing to recycle beyond the protected last card.

      See Also:
      • dealCard(boolean)
      • shuffle(Stack)

    • dealCard

      public Card dealCard(boolean faceUp)
      Deals the top card from the main deck with the specified face direction.

      If the main deck is empty, recycleDiscard() is called automatically to refill it from the discard pile before dealing.

      Parameters:
      faceUp - true to deal the card face up (human player); false to deal it face down (machine player)
      Returns:
      the top Card from the main deck
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if both the main deck and the discard pile are empty
      See Also:
      • recycleDiscard()

    • dealCard

      public Card dealCard()
      Deals the top card from the main deck face up.

      Convenience overload of dealCard(boolean) for the human player. Equivalent to calling dealCard(true).

      Returns:
      the top Card from the main deck, face up
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if both the main deck and the discard pile are empty

    • discard

      public void discard(Card card)
      Places a card onto the top of the discard pile, face up.

      This method should be called whenever a player plays a card during their turn. The card's faceUp state is set to true regardless of its previous state.

      Parameters:
      card - the Card to discard; must not be null

    • peekDiscard

      public Card peekDiscard()
      Returns the top card of the discard pile without removing it.

      Useful for displaying the current top of the discard pile in the UI without affecting the game state.

      Returns:
      the top Card of the discard pile, or null if the discard pile is empty

    • mainDeckSize

      public int mainDeckSize()
      Returns the number of cards remaining in the main deck.
      Returns:
      the current size of the main deck

    • discardPileSize

      public int discardPileSize()
      Returns the number of cards currently in the discard pile.
      Returns:
      the current size of the discard pile

    • isMainDeckEmpty

      public boolean isMainDeckEmpty()
      Returns true if the main deck contains no cards.
      Returns:
      true if the main deck is empty, false otherwise

    • isDiscardPileEmpty

      public boolean isDiscardPileEmpty()
      Returns true if the discard pile contains no cards.
      Returns:
      true if the discard pile is empty, false otherwise
